behavior_summary: U8P_ERTHREAG::look handles the 'U8P_ERTHREAG::look' (examine) event for ERTHREAG items: it builds a human-readable description string based on the item's frame/type and quantity, then emits that string via Item::bark so the player/process sees the description.

The routine queries the item for a frame/type code and a stack quantity, converts the numeric quantity to text, appends an appropriate noun/phrase selected by the frame (with singular/plural handling), frees temporary strings, and calls Item::bark(pid, description, item).

Different frame codes map to different noun phrases (e.g., vial(s) of blood, piles of bone shards/wood/dirt, executioner's hood variants, 'of blackmoor', 'deadman's elbow'), and the code branches on quantity to choose singular vs plural forms.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 0 (U8P_ERTHREAG::look) triggers the sequence: Item::getFrame -> Item::getQuantity -> numToStr -> string concatenations -> Item::bark.
A switch/branch on the frame/type value (local BP-02h) selects which suffix strings to append to the numeric base string.
Quantity (local BP-04h) is tested for singular vs plural to decide between singular noun forms and pluralized suffixes (often adding 's' or choosing an alternate suffix).
Temporary strings created during concatenation are freed before the final bark call to avoid leaks.
open_questions: Exact English mapping for every frame code: mappings are inferred from suffix literals in the disassembly but some frame-to-description mappings (edge cases, multi-byte frames) could be incomplete.
Precise semantics of the parameters passed to Item::bark (ordering and meaning of the pid vs item arguments) are inferred from calling patterns but not explicitly documented here.

Whether any additional side effects occur (e.g., setting item info flags) outside of string construction and barking; none observed in disassembly but could be implicit in opcodes not represented in the extraction.

main_function.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::look
main_function.purpose: When an actor/player examines an ERTHREAG item, produce a human-readable description describing the item's quantity and subtype (based on frame) and display it via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_ERTHREAG::look) — triggered when the avatar/actor examines the item (explicit event handled by the script).

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::look
function_map.role: Builds and emits the item's description when looked at (combines quantity and frame/type-driven noun selection).

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the item's frame/type code used to choose a descriptive suffix.

function_map.name: Item::getQuantity
function_map.role: Return the item's stack quantity used for numeric display and pluralization.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::numToStr
function_map.role: Convert a numeric quantity to its string representation as the base of the description.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::string concat (runtime opcode)
function_map.role: Concatenate strings to assemble the final description.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::string free (runtime opcode)
function_map.role: Free temporary strings created during concatenation.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display the final description string to the requesting process/player (bark from the item).

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's frame/type code to determine which descriptive noun/phrase to append.
Obtain the item's quantity for numeric display and to decide singular vs plural wording.
Convert the numeric quantity into a string used as the base of the description.
Append suffix strings (nouns, plural markers, 'of X') to the numeric base string to form the final message.
Free temporary/previous string buffers after concatenation to manage memory.
Display the completed description to the player/process that initiated the U8P_ERTHREAG::look.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ERTHREAG::look handles the 'U8P_ERTHREAG::look' (examine) event for ERTHREAG items: it builds a human-readable description string based on the item's frame/type and quantity, then emits that string via Item::bark so the player/process sees the description.
